source.title: Multimodal integration in granule cells as a basis for associative plasticity and sensory prediction in a cerebellum-like circuit

confidence_rationale: In vivo whole-cell recordings in mormyrid electric fish demonstrate the substrate-level mechanism by which a comparator circuit cancels predictable patterns of sensory input via combined sensory and motor mossy-fiber inputs. This is the canonical biological case study for the predictive-cancellation architecture that MECH-098 (reafference cancellation) instantiates and that SD-047 is intended to provide a non-trivial test environment for. Source quality high (Neuron, single-cell electrophysiology, in vivo). Mapping fidelity moderate because the substrate is fish electrosense rather than gridworld hazard fields, but the principle (combine motor + sensory to predict and subtract reafference) generalises.
failure_signatures: If MECH-098 in REE only ever sees the scheduled-hazard env (no continuous noise floor, no probabilistic transients), the cancellation mechanism never has a non-trivial prediction problem to solve -- the 'predictable patterns' Sawtell's circuit cancels are stochastic, autocorrelated, and continuously varying, not sparse scheduled events.

mapping.source_claim_statement: In cerebellum-like structures of mormyrid electric fish, granule cells integrate distinct sensory and motor mossy-fiber inputs, and Purkinje-like cells use anti-Hebbian plasticity at parallel-fiber synapses to generate 'negative images' that cancel predictable patterns of electrosensory input. Selective granule-cell encoding of sensory-motor combinations enhances the capacity for specific cancellation.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-098 (reafference cancellation) is REE's instantiation of this circuit. SD-047's spatial perturbation field and transient Poisson sources are substrate analogs of the continuous, stochastic, autocorrelated electrosensory noise floor against which Sawtell's negative-image circuit operates. The current V3 substrate (sparse scheduled hazards) provides nothing analogous -- there are no 'predictable patterns' to learn to cancel because the not-self causation is too sparse and too discrete.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Sawtell's substrate is electrosensory in fish; SD-047 substrate is hazard-intensity perturbation in a 2D grid world. The principle that transfers is: cancellation circuits require continuous, autocorrelated noise floors to learn against. The specific feature signatures (smoothness, autocorrelation timescale) are an empirical question -- the fish circuit is tuned to electrosensory statistics, REE's would need to be tuned to whatever statistics SD-047 produces.
mapping.source_context: In vivo whole-cell recordings in awake mormyrid electric fish performing electric organ discharges. Single-unit data from granule cells and Purkinje-like cells in the electrosensory lobe.

confidence_components.notes: Source_quality high (Neuron, in vivo electrophysiology, mature paradigm). Mapping_fidelity moderate -- the architectural principle (sensorimotor combination + anti-Hebbian for negative-image generation) transfers but the specific feature vocabulary (electrosense statistics) does not. Transfer_risk elevated for the specific feature claim, low for the architectural commitment.
